input.background: A lottery offers a grand prize of $10 million. The probability of winning this grand prize is 1 in 55 million (≈ 1.8 × 10^-8). There are no other prizes, so the probability of winning nothing = 1 - (1.8 × 10^-8) = 0.999999982. Table 5.5 shows the probability mass function for the problem.
input.question: Fifty-five million lottery tickets will be sold. How much does the proprietor of the lottery need to charge per ticket to make a profit?
